name: Jerrod Carmichael
bio: Jerrod Carmichael is an American comedian, actor, writer, and filmmaker who emerged from the Los Angeles club scene in the early 2010s. His HBO stand-up specials include Love at the Store, 8, Rothaniel, and Don't Be Gay; Rothaniel, in which he came out as gay, won the Emmy for Outstanding Writing for a Variety Special. He created and starred in the NBC sitcom The Carmichael Show and made his feature directing debut with On the Count of Three.

timeline.ev: Appears in the New Faces showcase at Just for Laughs
First HBO special, Love at the Store, directed by Spike Lee
The Carmichael Show premieres on NBC
Second HBO special, 8, directed by Bo Burnham
Rothaniel premieres; comes out as gay; hosts SNL

accolades: Emmy, Outstanding Writing for a Variety Special (Rothaniel, 2022)
WGA Award for Best Comedy/Variety Specials writing (Rothaniel)
